resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def compareVersion(self, version1: str, version2: str) -> int:
        v1 = list(map(int, version1.split('.')))
        v2 = list(map(int, version2.split('.')))
        n = max(len(v1), len(v2))
        for i in range(n):
            a = v1[i] if i < len(v1) else 0
            b = v2[i] if i < len(v2) else 0
            if a < b:
                return -1
            if a > b:
                return 1
        return 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(max(m, n)) where m and n are the lengths of the two version strings
space complexity: O(m + n) for the split lists
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Split each version string by '.' and convert segments to integers, then compare corresponding segments left-to-right, padding with 0 when one version is shorter. Early return on first difference keeps comparisons minimal.
